Company: Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison LLP
Text: I am writing in support of Rhea Singh, whom I have known in an individual and professional capacity for the past five years.  I first met Rhea while working at The Keys Academy Pte. Ltd.—an educational startup based in Singapore. She was among our promising admissions mentees, and I had the pleasure of working her over the course of a year on advancing her collegiate aspirations. I could tell right away that we had a uniquely talented student on our hands: she possessed abundant ambition and clarity of purpose, especially when it came to her goal of pursuing Law. She also displayed a mature understanding of her strengths and weaknesses and made an effort to amplify the former and mitigate the latter. These factors meant that it was effortless coaching her to become a superlative candidate for college—as evidenced by her acceptance to and exceptional performance at UC Berkeley.  Naturally, when Rhea approached us, seeking advice on securing a meaningful internship, we jumped at the chance to have her on-board. As an intern, Rhea was reliable and conscientious, quickly understanding professional expectations and acting on them accordingly. She displayed a positive attitude when interacting with clients and colleagues alike. Among her primary responsibilities was conducting extensive research to enhance our consulting knowledge base. Her work enabled us to provide better insights to our students, resulting in a competitive advantage for our company through improved productivity and higher client satisfaction. Rhea was also a key player in a push to better understand our market, providing salient insights from her time as a high school student in Singapore that shaped our approach to lead generation and client acquisition.          	Even more impressive was Rhea’s ‘can-do’ attitude. A talented writer, she quickly requested more involvement in the admissions consulting process. She took the initiative to shadow edit early drafts of essays, allowing senior consultants to take on a more significant workload. She also took ownership of our owned and shared media content, publishing over a dozen of articles and blog posts, raising week-on-week social media engagement by over 200%. Most of all, however, it was Rhea’s ability to put others at ease that impressed me the most. As a college student herself, she was able to supplement client interactions by contributing her experiences as a college student, helping resolve the impasses that naturally arise when mentoring students for college.  Rhea Singh would be an asset to any organization; I recommend her wholeheartedly and invite you to reach out should you have any questions about her time working at The Keys.   Vrijen Attawar Co-Founder and Former Managing Director The Keys Admissions Pte. Ltd.
